name: The two Castles - Castillo de Pioz and Castillo de Torremocha — circular through the valleys of the Río Tajuña
name_original: Los dos Castillos - Castillo de Pioz y Castillo de Torremocha — circular por los valles del Río Tajuña
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pioz traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, notably those of the Río Tajuña. The region features varied terrain, including ascents to viewpoints and historical sites such as Pioz Castle. Road cyclists can expect a mix of open countryside and routes passing through local villages. The elevation changes provide opportunities for both moderate and more challenging rides. Best road cycling routes around Pioz The most popular road cycling route is The two Castles - Castillo de Pioz and Castillo de Torremocha — circular through the valleys of the Río Tajuña, a 56.9 miles (91.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route features two historical castles and follows the valleys of the Río Tajuña. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Viewpoint of Los Santos de la Humosa and Ascent Albolleque — circular from Pioz, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.4 km) path. This route includes an ascent to Albolleque and offers views from Los Santos de la Humosa. Local road cyclists also love the Roadbike loop from Pozo de Guadalajara, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail leading through the countryside around Pozo de Guadalajara, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Pioz is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to advanced road cycling trails. The routes in Pioz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pioz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pioz?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Pioz, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Pioz?
A: Road cycling routes around Pioz feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills and river valleys, particularly those of the Río Tajuña. You'll encounter ascents to scenic viewpoints and historical sites, with routes passing through both open countryside and charming local villages.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pioz?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy', Pioz offers 10 moderate road cycling routes that are suitable for those with some cycling experience looking for a manageable challenge. These routes provide a good balance of distance and elevation without being overly strenuous.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders, Pioz offers 12 difficult road cycling routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015562638/the-two-castles-castillo-de-pioz-and-castillo-de-torremocha-circular-through-the-valleys-of-the-rio-tajuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The two Castles - Castillo de Pioz and Castillo de Torremocha — circular through the valleys of the Río Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 56.9 miles (91.6 km) with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24533055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road Along the Tajuña River – Ascent to Pastrana loop from Pioz&lt;/a&gt;, a 89.7 km route with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pioz?
A: Yes, many routes around Pioz are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015927467/viewpoint-of-los-santos-de-la-humosa-and-ascent-albolleque-circular-from-pioz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Los Santos de la Humosa and Ascent Albolleque — circular from Pioz&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33786919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Pozo de Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015562638/the-two-castles-castillo-de-pioz-and-castillo-de-torremocha-circular-through-the-valleys-of-the-rio-tajuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The two Castles - Castillo de Pioz and Castillo de Torremocha&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights two significant castles.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints on the road cycling trails in Pioz?
A: Yes, the region's rolling hills offer several scenic viewpoints. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015927467/viewpoint-of-los-santos-de-la-humosa-and-ascent-albolleque-circular-from-pioz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Los Santos de la Humosa and Ascent Albolleque&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to include stunning vistas. Many ascents throughout the area will reward you with panoramic views of the countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pioz?
A: The best time for road cycling in Pioz is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is generally milder, making for more comfortable rides. Summers can be hot, especially in the open countryside, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or rest stops along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, many routes pass through local villages where you can often find small shops or bars for refreshments. Additionally, you might encounter facilities like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1737135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Oliva fountain&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7267481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Rey Rest Area&lt;/a&gt;, which are useful for refilling water bottles.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pioz?
A: The road cycling routes in Pioz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the mix of open countryside and charming villages, and the opportunities to explore historical landmarks like Pioz Castle while enjoying a good workout.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Pioz?
A: Most road cycling routes around Pioz start and end in or near villages, where public parking is generally available. For routes originating directly from Pioz, you can typically find parking within the town itself, allowing for convenient access to the starting points.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling trails near Pioz?
A: Yes, Pioz offers several longer-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015562638/the-two-castles-castillo-de-pioz-and-castillo-de-torremocha-circular-through-the-valleys-of-the-rio-tajuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The two Castles - Castillo de Pioz and Castillo de Torremocha&lt;/a&gt; route is a substantial 91.6 km (56.9 miles) ride. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24533055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road Along the Tajuña River – Ascent to Pastrana loop from Pioz&lt;/a&gt;, which is 89.7 km long.
tips: Section of road that runs below the town of Corpa and from where you can see the church of Santo Domingo de Silos with its tall tower that dominates over all the other roofs!

Like the fortified archbishop's palace of Alcalá de Henares, this enclosure was ordered to be built by Archbishop Pedro Tenorio in the 14th century. It is a strong plaza with seven towers, a wall and the church of San Torcuato.

The church dedicated to San Pedro is from the 13th century but important reforms were carried out in it during the 15th century, the main nave was redone and two other lateral ones were added, for example. It preserves its original Romanesque head, the belfry and the presbytery. Inside, don't miss the Gothic ornaments, the Mudejar pair and knuckle armor; and the small ivory carving of the Virgen de los Llanos. It was declared B.I.C with the category of Monument in 1990.

Interesting work of the s. XV, which allows us to appreciate how a castle was arranged when the use of artillery had already been imposed: harquebuses and cannons. It is erected on a plain, and preserves both its interior and towers, as well as the outer enclosure and the moat.

Free access to the outside. To visit the interior, contact the Pioz town hall, telephone 949.272.076.
